scenario_results.reason: Inventory normalization guidance focused on data lineage, product normalization, and reconciliation rules without drifting into tool implementation.
SaaS reclamation guidance covered inactive-account controls, evidence retention, and exception handling while refusing identity-administration execution.
IBM audit-readiness response defined the operational proof set and escalation points without claiming a final licensing opinion.
Microsoft assignment hygiene review used official group-based licensing guidance to assess governance and reclamation readiness within the SAM boundary.
AWS License Manager review stayed at operating-model, evidence, and escalation guidance rather than cloud implementation detail.
Lifecycle controls analysis produced actionable governance improvements across request, approval, assignment, reclaim, and retirement stages.
Traceability review required replayable linkage between entitlement proofs, assignments, and install evidence and explicitly called out missing lineage.
Risk prioritization balanced shelfware, unsupported software, inventory quality, and missing entitlement evidence instead of raw spend only.
The package preserved a clean SAM versus software-licensing boundary by preparing evidence and routing final metric interpretation.
Negotiation and redlining work were rejected clearly with routing to procurement and legal owners.
Low-confidence handling requested the minimum viable inventory and entitlement data set rather than inventing risk or compliance conclusions.
Fallback behavior preserved the local reviewed baseline and avoided unsupported claims when delegated meta-agent outputs were stale.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against issue #127 requirements, including policy and controls analysis, commercial and licensing boundary handling, evidence and traceability quality, risk-based prioritization, low-confidence escalation, out-of-scope rejection, and shared-meta-agent fallback behavior.
No unreviewed regressions remain and human verification is recorded in readiness evidence.
